---
description: "Career profile template — copy this file, rename to career-profile-{YourName}.instructions.md, and fill in all placeholder values."
applyTo: "**"
---

# Career Profile — {Full Name}

## Contact Information

Always use these exact details in generated resumes and cover letters:

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Full Name** | {Full Name} |
| **Email** | {email@example.com} |
| **Phone** | {+XX XXX XXX XXXX} |
| **LinkedIn** | {https://www.linkedin.com/in/your-profile/} |
| **Location** | {City, Country} |

> Fill in all `{placeholder}` values before using this profile.
> This file is gitignored — it contains PII. Keep it local only.

## Target Roles

When matching skills or writing summaries, optimise for these role types:

- {Role 1 — e.g., Senior Data Analyst}
- {Role 2 — e.g., Platform Engineer}
- {Role 3}

## Target Industries

- {Industry 1 — e.g., FinTech}
- {Industry 2}

## Resume Constraints

Apply these rules to every resume generated:

1. **Max {2|3} pages** — trim bullets aggressively if needed; prioritise most recent and most relevant roles
2. **No references section** — never include "References available on request" or any references
3. **{Country} / {Region} format** — no photo, no date of birth, no marital status, no nationality
4. **ATS friendly** — no tables, no text boxes, no columns, no graphics; use standard section headings
5. **Right to Work: always include** — any work authorisation line is mandatory in every tailored resume

## Work Authorisation

{e.g., "EU citizen — no visa sponsorship required." or "Stamp 4 — full right to work in Ireland, no employer sponsorship needed."}

## Preferred Output Formats

Produce resumes in both:
- **Markdown** (`.md`) — using the [resume-tailor Markdown template](../skills/resume-tailor/assets/template-markdown.md)
- **LaTeX** (`.tex`) — using the [resume-tailor LaTeX template](../skills/resume-tailor/assets/template-latex.tex)
